message:
feat: wire remediation into check and add Docker integration tests

- Wire --remediate flag into check command failure path with
  ClaudeRemediation.execute_with_resilience()
- Fix parse_changes to parse JSON suggestions into FileChange structs
- Add 6 Docker integration tests (gated with DOCKER_TESTS env var)
- Close beads: 74o.7, 74o.6.1, 74o.6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -21,8 +21,8 @@
 {"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.4.1","title":"task: implement async URL checking for validate --check-urls","description":"Replace the stub in validate_checksums() that prints 'URL checking not implemented'.

Implementation:
- Use reqwest (already in Cargo.toml) to make HTTP HEAD requests to each installer URL
- Run checks concurrently with a semaphore (limit 10 concurrent requests)
- Timeout: 15s per URL (configurable)
- For each URL, report: installer_name, url, http_status, response_time_ms, reachable: bool
- Treat 200-299 as reachable, 301-399 as redirect (warn), 400+ as broken (error)
- Handle network errors (DNS failure, connection refused, timeout) as broken
- Human output: table with green checkmarks for reachable, red X for broken
- JSON output: array of {name, url, status, response_time_ms, error}

This function needs to be async. Options:
  a) Make validate_checksums async (breaking change to the sync API)
  b) Create a separate async fn check_urls() called from the CLI handler
  c) Use tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on() inside the sync fn

Recommend option (b): keep validate_checksums sync for basic validation, add a new
async check_urls() function called from cmd_validate when --check-urls is passed.

This is valuable for catching upstream URL changes before they break real installs.
ACFS has 41 URLs pointing to GitHub raw content, bun.sh, sh.rustup.rs, etc. — any
of these could change or go down.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:59.997122113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T22:06:43.529227341Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T22:06:43.528925065Z","close_reason":"Implemented async URL checking: concurrent HEAD requests with semaphore (10 concurrent), 15s timeout, reports status/response_time/reachability. Wired into validate --check-urls. All three output formats.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["network","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.4.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:24:59.997122113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5","title":"feature: implement real parallel execution with worker pool","description":"Parallel execution is a semaphore skeleton. Must implement a real worker pool using tokio tasks: spawn N workers (from --parallel flag), each pulls next installer from a channel/queue, runs it in its own Docker container, reports results back. Must handle: concurrent Docker container creation, proper cleanup on failure/timeout, progress reporting across workers, fail-fast mode (cancel remaining on first failure).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.167135055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T22:11:12.936257047Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T22:11:12.935956153Z","close_reason":"Child 74o.5.1 complete: parallel worker pool implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.167135055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5.1","title":"task: implement worker pool for parallel installer testing","description":"Replace the semaphore skeleton in runner/parallel.rs with a real worker pool.

Implementation using tokio mpsc channel pattern:
1. Create a channel: tx/rx for InstallerTest items
2. Spawn N worker tasks (N = --parallel flag, default 1)
3. Each worker loops: receive test from channel, call the executor (which dispatches
   to Docker or local mode based on config), send result back via results channel
4. Main task: send all tests to tx, then collect results from results rx
5. Progress reporting: print status line as each test completes (name, pass/fail, duration)

IMPORTANT: The worker pool calls the EXECUTOR abstraction, not Docker directly. This
means parallel execution works in both Docker mode and --local mode. Each worker gets
its own execution context (its own container in Docker mode, its own temp dir in local).

Fail-fast mode (--fail-fast):
- When a test fails, signal all workers to stop accepting new work
- Workers finish their current test but do not pick up new ones
- Use a shared AtomicBool or tokio CancellationToken

Concurrency safety:
- Each worker gets its own execution context (no shared state)
- Results channel handles ordering
- Progress output needs a mutex or single-writer pattern to avoid interleaved lines","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.766762158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T22:11:11.960273770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T22:11:11.959977105Z","close_reason":"Implemented real worker pool: tokio tasks with semaphore, fail-fast via AtomicBool, dispatches through executor abstraction (works with Docker and Local modes). Wired into check command when --parallel > 1.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["execution","parallel"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.888869300Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.766762158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6","title":"feature: wire up remediation and fix parse_changes stub","description":"The Claude CLI invocation path is real but: (1) _remediate is ignored in check handler — must wire it through. (2) parse_changes() at remediation/claude.rs returns vec![] — must actually parse Claude's output for actionable suggestions. (3) The remediation flow should: on failure → classify error → send context to Claude CLI → parse response → display suggestions (and optionally apply them if auto_commit is enabled). This is experimental/optional so lower priority, but the wiring must work end-to-end.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.242355474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.242355474Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:23:26.242355474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6.1","title":"task: wire remediation into check failure path and fix parse_changes stub","description":"Two things to fix:

1. WIRING: In the check command handler, after a test failure when --remediate is enabled:
   - Create a ClaudeRemediation instance
   - Call suggest() with the error context (installer name, stderr, exit code, classification)
   - Display the suggestions to the user
   - If auto_commit is enabled in config, attempt to apply suggestions

2. PARSE_CHANGES STUB: In remediation/claude.rs, parse_changes() returns vec![].
   Must parse Claude's response for actionable items. Expected format from Claude:
   - File modifications (path + diff/content)
   - Shell commands to run
   - Configuration changes
   
   Use a structured prompt that asks Claude to respond in a parseable format (JSON).
   The prompt template in remediation/prompts.rs is already reasonable — just need
   the parser to extract actions from Claude's response.

   Parsing approach: ask Claude to emit JSON with structure:
   {suggestions: [{type: 'fix'|'workaround'|'investigate', description: str,
     commands: [str], files: [{path, content}]}]}
   
   Then parse_changes() extracts the suggestions array.

This is P2 because the tool is useful without remediation — it's a nice-to-have
that adds value for automated repair workflows.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.966435139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.966435139Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude","remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:00.966435139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.7","title":"task: add integration tests for Docker container lifecycle","description":"Need real integration tests that verify Docker container management end-to-end.
Tests to add (skip if Docker unavailable - use #[ignore] + env var gate):

1. test_container_create_and_cleanup: create ubuntu:22.04, verify exists, cleanup, verify gone
2. test_exec_simple_command: exec echo hello, verify stdout=hello, exit_code=0
3. test_exec_failing_command: exec exit 42, verify exit_code=42
4. test_exec_timeout: exec sleep 300 with 2s timeout, verify timeout error, verify cleanup
5. test_container_resource_limits: create with memory_limit=256M, verify via inspect
6. test_container_environment_vars: exec env inside container, verify DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive,
   CI=true, NONINTERACTIVE=1 are set (validates non-interactive flags)
7. test_real_installer_in_container: run zoxide installer, verify exit_code=0, cleanup
8. test_container_network_access: exec curl -sI https://example.com, verify 200
9. test_container_naming_convention: create container for installer named rust,
   verify container name starts with afsc-rust-
10. test_container_cleanup_on_drop: create a container, drop the manager without explicit
    cleanup, verify the container is still removed (tests Drop / cancellation safety)

Gate with: #[cfg(feature = docker-tests)] or DOCKER_HOST env var check.
Each test should use tracing::info! for clear log output on failure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:30.153145415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:21:07.624417545Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docker","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.7","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:30.153145415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.7","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:30.603509404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","title":"task: add end-to-end test that runs 3-5 real ACFS installers and verifies results","description":"Add a bash E2E test script that exercises the full workflow:

1. Build the release binary
2. Run: afsc validate (should pass cleanly, zero warnings)
3. Run: afsc check zoxide atuin --parallel 2 --timeout 120
   - These are small, fast, reliable installers
   - Verify exit code 0
   - Verify human output shows 2 passed
4. Run: afsc check zoxide --format jsonl
   - Verify JSONL output is valid JSON per line
   - Verify status field is pass
   - Verify checksum_result.matches is true (SHA256 verification worked)
5. Run: afsc status
   - Verify it shows the most recent run (not No runs recorded yet)
   - Verify it lists the installers tested
6. Run: afsc list --format json
   - Verify all 41 installers are present
   - Verify each has url and sha256
7. Run: afsc check --local zoxide
   - Verify --local mode also works (no Docker needed for this step)
8. Verify exit code: run afsc check with a deliberately broken URL, verify non-zero exit

This test proves the entire tool works end-to-end against real ACFS data.
Add to scripts/e2e/tests/ alongside existing E2E tests.

Each step should log clearly: echo "=== STEP N: description ===" so failures are
easy to locate in CI  {"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","title":"task: update README to accurately reflect implemented vs planned features","description":"After all implementation is complete, do a final pass over README.md to ensure
every claim is accurate. The current README was written before the reality check and describes
some features as working that were actually stubs. Once the stubs are implemented, the README
should be accurate — but verify each claim against the actual code.

Specifically verify:
- Docker isolation claim matches real container usage
- Parallel execution claim matches real worker pool
- Error classification categories match the actual classifier
- Configuration options all actually work
- All command examples produce the shown output
- Architecture diagram matches the real code flow

This should be the LAST task — everything else must be done first.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:34.181570515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T18:44:54.242022042Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:34.181570515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:09:42.504499048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:09:43.237281383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:09:43.300772193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:29:07.976079110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:09:43.367712560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:44:54.241485328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:29:08.031633182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:29:08.087523192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:34.576331856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
